# GlobeVortex - Full Reference

## Identity
- Name: GlobeVortex
- URL: https://globevortex.com
- Type: AI-powered bilingual news aggregator
- Brand: DeepVortex
- Founded: 2026
- Contact: admin@globevortex.com
- Languages: French and English (FR/EN)

## Mission
GlobeVortex aims to be the most comprehensive international news aggregator, combining AI-powered curation with global RSS coverage. Bilingual FR/EN, updated every 15 minutes, powered by Claude AI.

## How it works
1. Every 15 minutes, a GitHub Actions cron job fetches RSS feeds from 80+ international sources
2. Each article is processed by Claude Haiku AI: classified by category, summarized in 2-3 sentences, and given an importance score (0-100)
3. Articles are stored in Supabase (PostgreSQL) with full metadata
4. The Next.js frontend reads from Supabase and displays articles with ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration)
5. Top Stories = articles with importance score >= 60 from the last 24 hours

## Importance Scoring (Claude Haiku)
- 90-100: Global breaking news (wars, elections, major disasters)
- 75-89: Major national or international events
- 50-74: Regional or notable events
- 0-49: Routine news

## News Sources (sample)
BBC News, BBC Technology, BBC Health, BBC Business, BBC Football, CNN Business, Sky Sports, franceinfo, RFI, RFI Sport, Le Figaro International, Radio-Canada, Le Devoir, TVA Nouvelles, Journal de Montreal, L'Actualite, and 65+ more international sources

## Article Data Structure
Each article contains: title, AI-generated summary, source name, source URL, category, language (EN/FR), importance score (0-100), published date, image URL

## Pages and URLs
- https://globevortex.com - Home, Top Stories, all categories
- https://globevortex.com/about - About page bilingual FR/EN
- https://globevortex.com/article/[uuid] - Individual article detail page with NewsArticle JSON-LD
- https://globevortex.com/sitemap.xml - XML sitemap with all article URLs

## Structured Data
Every article page uses Schema.org NewsArticle JSON-LD. The home page uses NewsMediaOrganization and WebSite schemas.
